tool_args.label: Webster Avenue

payload.description: Webster Avenue corridor in the Bronx, from approximately East 138th Street/3rd Avenue area north to beyond 204th Street, including intersections with East Tremont Avenue, Fordham Road, Bedford Park Boulevard, East Gun Hill Road, Mosholu Parkway, and Cross Bronx Expressway.

tool_args.label: Bx41

payload.description: Bus route serving Webster Avenue; service to East 138th Street noted as needing restoration.

tool_args.label: Bx12 SBS

payload.description: Bx12 Select Bus Service on Fordham Road, cited as example of SBS results.

tool_args.label: Webster Avenue SBS CAC Meeting #2

payload.event_name: Webster Avenue Select Bus Service Community Advisory Committee Meeting #2
payload.description: Second Community Advisory Committee meeting for the Webster Avenue Select Bus Service project, held at Icahn Charter Middle School Cafeteria, 1506 Brook Avenue, Bronx, NY.

tool_args.label: Curbside Bus Lane Alignment

payload.description: Conceptual curbside bus lane alignment for Webster Avenue SBS. Advantages: maintains 2 travel lanes, familiar alignment. Disadvantages: loss of parking, people walk in bus lane, high number of violators reduce effectiveness.

tool_args.label: Offset Bus Lane Alignment

payload.description: Conceptual offset bus lane alignment for Webster Avenue SBS. Advantages: easier for deliveries, possibility of neck downs and pedestrian islands, no loss of parking, people waiting for bus do not block sidewalk, better for buses than curbside, can add landscaping. Disadvantages: only one lane of traffic in each direction, conflicts between buses and cars as cars move across bus lane to park, right turns problematic.

tool_args.label: Median Bus Lane Alignment

payload.description: Conceptual median bus lane alignment for Webster Avenue SBS. Advantages: median islands shorten crossing distance at bus stops, better in snow, faster buses, people can easily find SBS stop, better for buses than curbside, can add greenery at stations. Disadvantages: only one lane of traffic in each direction, seniors may not feel safe standing in middle of road, banned left turns at some intersections, safety concerns, loss of parking at stations, forces choice between local and SBS.

tool_args.label: East Tremont and Webster dangerous intersection

payload.claim_text: East Tremont Avenue and Webster Avenue is one of the most dangerous intersections on the corridor due to the high volume of traffic, buses, and school children. The right turn from East Tremont Avenue to southbound Webster Avenue is especially dangerous.
payload.subject: East Tremont Avenue and Webster Avenue intersection

tool_args.label: Bx41 service to East 138th Street needs restoration

payload.claim_text: Very important to serve public housing at East 138th Street and 3rd Avenue. Bx41 bus service to East 138th Street needs to be restored.
payload.subject: Bx41 bus service

warnings: claim.route is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Route ids should link to route records when supported. Suggested relation: route has_claim claim when the route endpoint resolves exactly.

tool_args.label: Congestion on Webster Avenue at key intersections

payload.claim_text: Congestion on Webster Avenue approaching the following streets: Cross Bronx Expressway, E Tremont Road, E Fordham Road, E Gun Hill Road, Bedford Park Boulevard (livery cab stand often has double and triple parked cars).
payload.subject: Webster Avenue congestion

tool_args.label: Webster Avenue rezoning and planned developments

payload.claim_text: Webster Avenue was recently rezoned between Fordham Road and Gun Hill Road. Plans for 100,000 square feet of commercial development near the New York Botanical Garden, including a hotel. Large residential developments are planned near Webster Avenue and E Gun Hill Road. A 140-unit development is slated for East 211th Street.
payload.subject: Webster Avenue corridor development

tool_args.label: Rider preference overall conclusion

payload.claim_text: Participants preferred the 'medium' scenario (five minutes walking, five minutes waiting, twenty minute trip) over the other two scenarios, which offered a shorter bus trip time with a longer walk time or a shorter walk and wait time but longer bus trip time.
payload.subject: rider preference exercise
payload.description: Overall conclusion from the rider preference workshop activity at the Webster Avenue SBS CAC Meeting #2
